zh.Kilkhampton Manor, Stowe, Cornwall, Engl: 英國可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓

zh.Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England: 英國可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy Vyvyan Lady於大約1248年出生在英國可瓦爾時，她的父親Sir Vyell Vyvyan45歲，母親Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare45歲。她於1282年與Sir Bartholomew de Grenville在英國英格蘭可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1344年在她的家鄉享年97歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy Vyvyan Lady于大约1248年出生在英国可瓦爾时，她的父亲Sir Vyell Vyvyan45岁，母亲Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare45岁。她于1282年与Sir Bartholomew de Grenville在英国英格兰可瓦爾吉爾哈普頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1344年在她的家乡享年97岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vyvyan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Vivian, Vivien, from Latin Vivianus (derivative of vivus ‘living’), the name of a 5th-century Christian martyr. As a personal name its comparative rarity in medieval England gave it a distinctiveness that encouraged its use as a surname. Its pronunciation appears to have caused difficulty and it is found in a large variety of forms, not all of which have survived. Developments include the interchange of initial V and F, the interchange of intervocalic v and th, and the further interchange of th and d; thus Vivian became Fivian, which became Fithian, which became Fidian. See also Fithen .
French: mainly southern variant of the personal name Vivien (see 1 above).
Spanish and Galician (Vivián): from the personal name Vivián (see 1 above). Compare Bibian .

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Amy Vyvyan Lady was born about 1248, in Cornwall, England, her father, Sir Vyell Vyvyan, was 45 and her mother, Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, was 45. She married Sir Bartholomew de Grenville in 1282, in Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1344, in her hometown, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amy Vyvyan Lady est née environ 1248, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Sir Vyell Vyvyan, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Bartholomew de Grenville en 1282, à Kilkhampton, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1344, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Amy Vyvyan Lady um 1248 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Vyell Vyvyan 45 und ihre Mutter Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare 45. Sie hat 1282 in Kilkhampton, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom Sir Bartholomew de Grenville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1344 im Alter von 97 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Amy Vyvyan Lady è nata il circa 1248 nel Cornwall, England, sua padre, Sir Vyell Vyvyan, aveva 45 anni e sua madre, Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Sir Bartholomew de Grenville nel 1282, nel Kilkhampton, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1344, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 97 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy Vyvyan Ladyが約1248年にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父Sir Vyell Vyvyanは45歳，母Margaret FitzGerald of Kildareは45歳であった。1282年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルキルクハンプトンでSir Bartholomew de Grenvilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1344年に故郷で97歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Vyvyan Lady(이)가 대략 1248년 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Vyell Vyvyan(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1282년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 킬카프턴에서 Sir Bartholomew de Grenville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 그녀의 고향에서 97세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Amy Vyvyan Lady nasceu aproximadamente 1248, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Vyell Vyvyan, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Bartholomew de Grenville em 1282, em Kilkhampton, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1344, em sua cidade natal, com 97 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy Vyvyan Lady родилась около 1248 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Vyell Vyvyan было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Bartholomew de Grenville в 1282 г. в местности килхамптон, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1344 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 97 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy Vyvyan Lady nació aproximadamente 1248, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Vyell Vyvyan, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Margaret FitzGerald of Kildare, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Sir Bartholomew de Grenville en 1282, en Kilkhampton, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1344, en su pueblo, a la edad de 97 años.
